content.text: How can I correctly merge a set of user-provided parameters with the default parameters defined within my request builder?

This method manages parameter merging by first creating a safe copy of your input data, then injecting any predefined defaults held internally.

```python
def with_params(self, params):
        '''Adds parameters to the request params

        Args:
            params (dict): The parameters to add to the request params

        Returns:
            The request builder instance in order to chain calls
        '''
        copy = params.copy()
        copy.update(self._params)
        return self.__copy_and_set('params', copy)
```

Note that since `self._params` is updated onto the copied dictionary, internal default settings will overwrite user inputs if both dictionaries contain the same keys.
If I pass an empty dictionary for params, does it ensure all of my service defaults are still applied?
Yes. If you call `with_params({})`, there is no external data to conflict with; thus, all parameters from your instance's internal list (`self._params`) will be added directly.
I feel much better about the merging logic now!
